title: Single-nuclei suspensions from primary human esophagus tissue
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"cjt67","text":"A protocol to dissociate frozen esophagus tissue specimens for single-nuclei transcriptomics.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start:
{"blocks":[{"key":"3r5kb","text":"RNALater-ICE pre-treatment of frozen tissue (Important: perform on at least the day before nuclei isolation):","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":45,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7hv2a","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4d6m4","text":"Pre-chill a minimum of 10 volumes (compared to the sample mass/ volume) of RNAlater-ICE at 70 or -80°C in a polypropylene tube.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":97,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1ar1l","text":"Place frozen tissue (from LN2 or -80°C), no larger than 0.5 cm in the smallest dimension, into the RNAlater-ICE. It is important that the RNAlater-ICE stays cold and the tissue remains frozen, so work quickly. Set up the tubes of RNAlater-ICE and tissue samples in a container of dry ice. For the frozen tissue, simply pipet at least 10 volumes of pre-chilled RNAlater-ICE into the tube. Tightly cap the tube and invert several times to mix. ","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":32,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"drbd1","text":"Soak the tissue in RNAlater-ICE at -20°C for at least 16 hours. Samples transitioned to -20°C in RNAlater-ICE should be left in the RNAlater-ICE and maintain at -20°C or colder.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":34,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":87,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":161,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e723f","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6inmp","text":"Reagents to prepare immediately before use:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6s80","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b5vvf","text":"Prepare 6 mL of Nuclei Suspension Buffer (NSB; consisting of 1x PBS, 0.01% BSA and 0.2% v/v RNAse
inhibitor, 1 mM DTT). Keep cold on ice.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1mbk8","text":"Prepare 7 mL of Nuclei EZ lysis buffer (0.2% v/v RNAse inhibitor, 0.2% v/v SUPERasin, 1x Protease inhibitor
and 4 mM DTT). Keep cold on ice.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"48rao","text":"Preparing the 300 nM DAPI solution:","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"82viu","text":"            i) Add 2 mL of ddH20 to the DAPI vial to make a 14.3 mM (5 mg/mL) stock solution. Store at -20C.
            ii)  Add 2.1 uL of the 14.3 mM DAPI stock solution to 100 uL PBS to make a 300 μM DAPI intermediate dilution.
            iii)  Dilute the 300 μM DAPI intermediate dilution 1:1000 in PBS as needed to make a 300 nM DAPI solution.","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f9cao","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"62a89","text":"NB: Clean benchtop area with RNA-Zap and wipe down pipettes. Keep all reagents on ice at all times.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":3}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Pre-chill Dounce homogenizer with pestle B, containing 2 ml of ice-cold Nuclei EZ lysis buffer.

Remove tissue from storage (-20°C in RNALater-ICE) and hold in a petri dish on wet ice. If not utilizing all of the frozen tissue, sever off the amount of tissue required (using a cold scalpel) and return the remainder to original storage site.
Using a cold scalpel cut tissue into very small pieces and transfer to Dounce homogeniser. Do not overload homogeniser with tissue (~30 mg per Dounce at one time).

Homogenize for 30 strokes with pestle B (pre-chilled in ice-cold in Nuclei EZ lysis buffer. Tissue should be reduced to ~90% homogenous small particles. [Perform the homogenization on ice, occasionally removing from the ice to check the homogenate].

With a 1 mL pipet, transfer homogenate to 15 mL conical centrifuge tube (on ice) and rinse the Dounce homogenizer out with a further 1 mL of ice-cold Nuclei EZ lysis buffer. Take care not to transfer any large particulates of unhomogenized tissue. This is a potential source of activated RNAses that have undergone renaturation. Incubate on ice for 5 minutes.
Filter suspension through a 40 μM cell strainer in to a 50 ml conical centrifuge tube.
Centrifuge nuclei/tissue suspension at 700 x g at 4°C for 5 minutes.
Re-suspend isolated nuclei in 4 ml of ice-cold Nuclei EZ lysis buffer and incubate on ice for a further 5 minutes.
Centrifuge nuclei suspension at 500 x g at 4°C for 5 minutes.
Re-suspend nuclei in 3 mL NSB and filter through a 30 μM strainer in to a 50 mL conical centrifuge tube.
Centrifuge nuclei suspension at 500 x g at 4°C for 5 minutes.
Re-suspend nuclei in 1-2 mL NSB and hold on wet ice (4°C).

Dilute 10 μL of nuclei suspension 1 in 10 with NSB in to a micro-centrifuge tube and count nuclei using 1:2 dilution with dye (either 10 μl Trypan blue or 10 μl DAPI stain (300 nM in PBS; incubate for 2 minutes with nuclei before counting) added to 10 μl of nuclei suspension) and a haemocytometer (Improved Neubauer).
creator: Matthew JJ Rose Zerilli
